clean_body: Press coverage of Judicial Watch report on Biden dog biting Secret Service agents [NEWSWATCH] KEY RELEASE [600x300] [] Records Reveal Multiple Attacks on Secret Service by Biden’s Dog, Agents Upset with Psaki’s Misleading Statements About an Incident [] Source: Judicial Watch [] Judicial Watch obtained 400 pages of records detailing multiple attacks and damages caused to Secret Service members by President Biden’s dog Major at the White House and Biden’s lake home in Wilmington, DE. The documents reveal that a member of the Secret Service who was attacked by the dog was displeased that White House Press Secretary Jen Psaki misled the press about the incident. [13x13x1] [] READ MORE [] MAJOR MEDIA REPORTS [180x150x1] [] 2022-04-14 Bidens’ dog Major involved in other attacks on Secret Service agents [] Source: The Washington Times [] Secret Service agents were angered by the White House’s efforts to downplay dog-bite injuries last year from President Biden’s German shepherd, Major, newly released documents obtained by the conservative watchdog group Judicial Watch show. [13x13x1] [] READ MORE [] [180x150x2] [] 2022-04-13 ‘Now I’m pissed’: Secret Service agents outraged by White House spin over ‘first dog’ bites [] Source: New York Post [] Secret Service agents were outraged last year by the White House’s attempt to downplay bite injuries caused by then-first dog Major — even trying to get President Biden to personally pay for a damaged coat, newly released documents show. [13x13x1] [] READ MORE [] [180x150x3] [==] 2022-04-14 Secret Service agents were furious at the Biden administration's bid to downplay first dog Major's dog bites before he was given away [==] Source: Daily Mail [==] Secret Service agents were furious that the Biden administration tried to downplay former first dog Major's bites before he was rehomed with family friends. The conservative watchdog group Judicial Watch released documents concerning Major's biting incidents at both the White House and President Biden's Delaware home. Take a look at some of our campaign highlights from the past few days. --- | | --- | --- Last Tuesday Brett spent the day at the Unicameral fighting to cut taxes and was able to make it to a Meet and Greet in Wahoo that night while the #LadiesForLindstrom held down the fort in Omaha. --- --- Wednesday, [Brett shared his vision of lower taxes and economic development at the Omaha Chamber of Commerce Gubernatorial Candidate forum. ](http://url5297.votelindstrom.com/ls/click?upn=HTMZIboFmIG-2BMNluIWzOsMZ-2BsY2lBenqXUeh740ZV7tfFX6vNHMPxb7GWw1iguzjdBYfp5F7iBSAn0BZqe5jzs-2FqAwm-2BPZmPDpkGUmlOD88OTNg6MfsR5Ws-2FWD4yk6KW-2F3JZUU6-2FyY5HZTH3l0Bto23ehhgSubP-2BuM0ILgtUa-2BR8ht6nTEVnikrCiIy0ez2X6oS5Xob-2Fh-2FtQ5ditXEEUxw-3D-3Dt3th_XwU9lPq5-2Bze3gI64ohlV0cQwWAuBAU7ADIvj2CS7ymhuTI2jG3IYR5NWbV52qgmdAiGhBklNQsRXTWl4rWtz6V26ifmsOhc2lTNkdXy-2F5FFomLfxGZOGa3bZcww1pfz6H9em-2BSOdMixNlzERyKy-2FZr4IjPk09V3HDZM0oLztFlzHWyLL-2BCdZEa2Ck1tZClnFTEXRFHT6HtkbG-2BmdSevLRgrzv9u79NY0kjnlVKqIMf3vIckZjU9V74n3YoVmIdG0H0GcSWGWwKWCs0k7OtQmtxX6mt-2FgkVK4NflqOZODF06mgkofSShJ8r0DZuTWXdntkQGARq1CqU78OYi8gdiu-2BGYN2uSyZDjsgnlZk3fXzvFS1-2FBfXBPLQRihIjigNiPz-2Bt5SlhTWi6QsElsCi6DoiR0GlRI2IjoT8YgogX8BsUmPJayHdbwh1Tdusb2qqV1h9kkfS7qrKfdVNq16AoyhLeNTaiyTy-2BbWHw0QFOipzUN1HJTDFD6hCmYBAKuCth7wMc4yduUz9vvq9JQW2OkCgZMhsjmVHSf3MpStS2ocJ-2Ft66p6HLfIyxyItp9Q574xHfUX9yKN7iGoScyTs9J-2FNBXd8PW1iPbYM4Kj6d2vl4V8HvKjAF-2BaV6QEsnTeEFuM7p4TAPIFxvU-2FG8OBIixV0Z1bqC1N7EmXKHs4-2BP5QM100BzMO3GXXwtTIeSFZtV9UrFnG5m99aAk6GxgzSHWL73AKQVNPd6-2BzD6o1SCTgG8K33NQEKdmFG51cYtAiLAWJfEBcJpG2SaaR0C1xX0Sfn0O81nNMCoddXYHHOHjzP3kdXJ1Wo3BXUDw3sXMMn-2BXLZSYAGrimEaZq5mgbNoKQ9-2FAisgGduYnt0zaAU-2B66nc0sgEL7dMqS4-2BTYv3btNVepTdb7agGjJAHAG7dpnBgMPA0F5yWVKF3BEDfbCd2h6rcyTjckwjqVZDYHh2G9Kt6BJb5-2FEXXgTkfa5-2FVA62oVYuA-3D-3D "Brett shared his vision of lower taxes and economic development at the Omaha Chamber of Commerce Gubernatorial Candidate forum. ") After that he raced back to Lincoln to cast as crucial pro-life vote and then it was off to end the day at a packed event in Lincoln! --- --- Thursday and Friday saw Brett in the Unicameral fighting for life, lower taxes, and a brighter future in Nebraska. --- | | --- | --- | --- Team Lindstrom was out in force at the Huskers Spring game this weekend. We met a lot of fired up Brett supporters and got to enjoy some great football. Go Big Red! --- | | --- | --- After the Spring Game, Brett also had the privilege of showing his three kids the Nebraska State Capitol. --- | | --- Brett toured Western Nebraska for several days this week, starting with a PACKED meet and greet in Gering Tuesday evening. Every corner of the state is responding to Brett’s message and the momentum on the ground is AMAZING! --- | | --- | --- | --- | --- After that, Brett was up early for a breakfast in North Platte followed by a great meet and greet in Cozad. The response to Brett’s positive and conservative message has been fantastic across the state! --- | | --- | --- | --- On his way back east Brett toured First Street Brewing Company and met with the Hasting’s Young Professionals to talk about his economic development plan. --- | | --- | --- This weekend Team Lindstrom was all over the state knocking doors and putting up yard signs. In the closing weeks of the primary it is all hands on deck. We need your help! From hosting events, to knocking doors and delivering yard signs, there are so many different ways you can help get Brett over the finish line. clean_body: | | | | BIG TAX CUTS and even bigger MOMENTUM! Friends, We are 25 days out from the Primary Election and the Lindstrom surge continues! Take a look at some of our campaign highlights from the past few days. | | | Last Tuesday Brett spent the day at the Unicameral fighting to cut taxes and was able to make it to a Meet and Greet in Wahoo that night while the #LadiesForLindstrom held down the fort in Omaha. Wednesday, "Brett shared his vision of lower taxes and economic development at the Omaha Chamber of Commerce Gubernatorial Candidate forum. " After that he raced back to Lincoln to cast as crucial pro-life vote and then it was off to end the day at a packed event in Lincoln! Thursday and Friday saw Brett in the Unicameral fighting for life, lower taxes, and a brighter future in Nebraska. | | | | Team Lindstrom was out in force at the Huskers Spring game this weekend. We met a lot of fired up Brett supporters and got to enjoy some great football. Go Big Red! | | | After the Spring Game, Brett also had the privilege of showing his three kids the Nebraska State Capitol. | | Brett toured Western Nebraska for several days this week, starting with a PACKED meet and greet in Gering Tuesday evening. Every corner of the state is responding to Brett’s message and the momentum on the ground is AMAZING! | | | | | After that, Brett was up early for a breakfast in North Platte followed by a great meet and greet in Cozad. The response to Brett’s positive and conservative message has been fantastic across the state! | | | | On his way back east Brett toured First Street Brewing Company and met with the Hasting’s Young Professionals to talk about his economic development plan. | | | This weekend Team Lindstrom was all over the state knocking doors and putting up yard signs. In the closing weeks of the primary it is all hands on deck. We need your help! From hosting events, to knocking doors and delivering yard signs, there are so many different ways you can help get Brett over the finish line. If you would like us to deliver a yard sign or a 4x8 sign at your property, business, or residence -- please click the button below, and I will be in touch shortly. | We are thankful for your support and truly can't do this without you. Have a great rest of the week and we hope to see you soon on the trail! All the Best, Tori Midkiff Political Director Lindstrom for Governor | | "Facebook" | "Twitter" ---|--- Paid for by Friends of Lindstrom Campaign - 2433 S. 130th Circle, Omaha, NE, 68144

name: Jessica Hamilton, Evergreen Action
subject: Raise standards for fossil fuels [PUBLIC COMMENT]
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY EVERGREEN ACTION
body:
The Federal Energy Regulatory Commission needs to hear from us.
| [](https://act.evergreenaction.com/letter/tell-FERC-fossil-fuels-higher-standard/?utm_source=ema_2022-04-15_FERC_comment_general_&source=ema_2022-04-15_FERC_comment_general_&t=2&akid=12963%2E53494%2EjwCVFA) | _This email is part of Evergreen's newest policy push: our**Powering Towards 100% Clean Power** campaign. Together, we'll mobilize support to win critical, strategic victories for our clean energy future.
Interested? [_**Act today to stay in the loop!**_](https://act.evergreenaction.com/letter/tell-FERC-fossil-fuels-higher-standard/?utm_source=ema_2022-04-15_FERC_comment_general_&source=ema_2022-04-15_FERC_comment_general_&t=3&akid=12963%2E53494%2EjwCVFA)_
---
Friend,
Even as the climate crisis accelerates and harms frontline communities, Big Polluters are relentlessly pushing for new fossil fuel projects, despite the damage they would cause.
We must consider the climate and environmental justice impacts of all major infrastructure projects— _especially_ fossil fuel infrastructure. And right now, we have a historic opportunity to make that happen.
For the first time, the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC)—the agency in charge of providing the nation with safe, reliable, and affordable energy—is considering new policies to hold Big Polluters to climate and equity standards.
[_**Will you sign an official public comment supporting FERC's new rules? We'll submit it on your behalf directly to agency decision-makers!**_](https://act.evergreenaction.com/letter/tell-FERC-fossil-fuels-higher-standard/?utm_source=ema_2022-04-15_FERC_comment_general_&source=ema_2022-04-15_FERC_comment_general_&t=4&akid=12963%2E53494%2EjwCVFA)
[SIGN ON](https://act.evergreenaction.com/letter/tell-FERC-fossil-fuels-higher-standard/?utm_source=ema_2022-04-15_FERC_comment_general_&source=ema_2022-04-15_FERC_comment_general_&t=5&akid=12963%2E53494%2EjwCVFA)
---
FERC was established when oil and coal were the main energy sources powering our country. Everything was designed to favor fossil fuels—but now, we need to level the playing field for clean energy.
These new rules would reveal how Big Polluters' projects would contribute to the climate crisis, as well as help protect the frontline communities that would otherwise be harmed by these projects.
FERC has a major role to play in transitioning our country to cheaper, more reliable clean energy. It starts right here, right now—we must make sure FERC follows through.
[_**Will you raise your voice alongside thousands of fellow clean energy advocates? Just sign your name and we'll submit your official public comment directly to FERC.**_](https://act.evergreenaction.com/letter/tell-FERC-fossil-fuels-higher-standard/?utm_source=ema_2022-04-15_FERC_comment_general_&source=ema_2022-04-15_FERC_comment_general_&t=6&akid=12963%2E53494%2EjwCVFA)
[SIGN ON](https://act.evergreenaction.com/letter/tell-FERC-fossil-fuels-higher-standard/?utm_source=ema_2022-04-15_FERC_comment_general_&source=ema_2022-04-15_FERC_comment_general_&t=7&akid=12963%2E53494%2EjwCVFA)
---
Together,
Jessica Hamilton
Digital Campaign Manager, Evergreen Action
P.S. It's nice to meet you! I'm Evergreen's new digital campaign manager, and you'll be hearing more from me about ways you can make a real difference. Thanks for being a climate advocate!
[ADD YOUR NAME](https://act.evergreenaction.com/letter/tell-FERC-fossil-fuels-higher-standard/?utm_source=ema_2022-04-15_FERC_comment_general_&source=ema_2022-04-15_FERC_comment_general_&t=8&akid=12963%2E53494%2EjwCVFA)
---
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. [Click here to unsubscribe](https://act.evergreenaction.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=9&akid=12963%2E53494%2EjwCVFA). Contributions or gifts to EVERGREEN ACTION are not tax deductible. | PAID FOR BY EVERGREEN ACTION
---

name: Josh Gottheimer
subject: Happy Holidays
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JOSH GOTTHEIMER FOR CONGRESS
body:
# [#]Before Passover and Easter begin, I wanted to take a moment to wish you happy holidays and express my thoughts.

Whatever holiday you celebrate, both Passover and Easter serve as a much-needed reminder of hope, perseverance, and cause for celebration.

In a time of extreme political discord and uncertainty, we must strive to work together as one people, one nation.

We know the NRCC and dark money groups won’t let up, but I know we are stronger than they are when we work together. Thank you for being a part of this team.

I hope you and your family are well and enjoy the holidays if you celebrate.

Yours,

Josh
Josh Gottheimer represents New Jersey’s Fifth Congressional District. Josh is in Congress to fight for lower taxes and Jersey Values. He’s working across the aisle to find real solutions for New Jersey families and is actively fighting against the ideological gridlock in Washington. name: Gabby Giffords
subject: Mark is my best friend
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rosen for Nevada
body:
If he's going to win re-election, he needs your help.
| | |
---
Friend –
Mark is my best friend, my partner, and my rock. I feel so lucky to have him by my side every day.
He has helped me through my darkest moments and he always fights for what is right.
At war, he flew in combat, and at peace, he joined NASA and commanded the space shuttle twice.
Mark never planned to be a Senator, but he knows when it’s time to serve. He’s served his family, our country, and now the great state of Arizona.
But if he’s going to win reelection, he needs your help. And the next couple of hours are critical. Because tonight at midnight, we end our $50,000 fundraising drive. We need a strong showing today to catch up to where we need to be.

body:
RiverRocks Concert for John Selker!

Where?
Corvallis: Central Park for this live concert

When?
2:00 pm to 4:30 pm, Saturday, April 16, 2022

Bonus: Wooden lathe turned bowls (fundraiser), made by John Selker

Cause: Dr. John Selker is running for Oregon's 4th District open seat BECAUSE THERE ARE CURRENTLY NO EARTH SCIENTISTS IN CONGRESS. Learn about his candidacy at a table nearby, listen to some rockin'-the-folk-out-of-it music in the park, and help us raise money to get the vote out.

Candidate: Dr. John Selker will have information about his candidacy for Oregon's 4th District, and will say a few words between sets
Check out [Selker4congress.org](http://selker4congress.org/)

name: Democratic Senate Update
subject: WE WON
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Abby for Iowa Abby for Iowa
body:
REPUBLICANS JUST LOST. AGAIN.
| | |
---
| | **National Republicans tried _twice_ to kick Democrat Abby Finkenauer off the ballot in Iowa.**

**REPUBLICANS JUST LOST._AGAIN. _**

**\- INDems Welcomes Secretary Pete Back Home, Celebrates Jobs Act’s Investment for Tell City -** This week, Indiana Democrats celebrated the return of Secretary Pete Buttigieg to the Hoosier State as he touted how President Joe Biden’s Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act (The Jobs Act) will provide $1.5 million to revitalize Tell City’s port infrastructure system. Buttigieg also made the case on how The Jobs Act and overall economic agenda by Democrats is delivering for rural communities across the nation – including for Indiana’s rural communities. “It’s always a good day to welcome Secretary Pete Buttigieg ‘back home again to Indiana.' There's much to celebrate today, because Indiana Democrats like André Carson and Frank Mrvan delivered a once-in-a-generation investment to our state’s infrastructure, and thanks to The Jobs Act, Tell City’s economy (and all of our Hoosier river towns) will have a brighter future,” **said Chairman Mike Schmuhl.** “Whether it’s combating inflation or investing in rural America, President Joe Biden and Democrats are solving today’s biggest problems facing Hoosier families. Should any Indiana Republican join Secretary Pete Buttigieg, it will be for a photo op because not a single one of them supported Tell City’s infrastructure funding. They let their partisanship come before this brighter future for Perry County.” **_Not one_ Indiana Republican in the state’s Congressional delegation supported The Jobs Act – including U.S. Congressman Larry Bucshon.** In fact, politicians like Bucshon called this port investment part of a “wave of socialism”. Indiana GOP Chairman Kyle Hupfer even said opposing infrastructure investments were a “great campaign to run on”. The Indiana GOP has no plan for Indiana’s future – just narrow partisanship. **\- Indiana GOP Caught Violating Election Laws Three Times in One Year – Despite Partisan Agenda -** The State Party also criticized the Indiana Republican Party for blatantly violating election laws three times in just a year. These errors come despite the Indiana GOP [passing an “election integrity” law](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/44182856/340449719/1032135708?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhZWQ1Y2RlNi1mMGJjLWVjMTEtOTk3ZS0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Jnmu5QrUlFpMiPPQASRyfMxUeq5mU-6QndaLO-UCSVQ=&emci=7234f70d-e8bc-ec11-997e-281878b83d8a&emdi=aed5cde6-f0bc-ec11-997e-281878b83d8a&ceid=4925535) during this year’s session and politicians like Todd Rokita [claiming the 2020 presidential election was stolen](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/44182857/340449720/1482943504?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhZWQ1Y2RlNi1mMGJjLWVjMTEtOTk3ZS0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Jnmu5QrUlFpMiPPQASRyfMxUeq5mU-6QndaLO-UCSVQ=&emci=7234f70d-e8bc-ec11-997e-281878b83d8a&emdi=aed5cde6-f0bc-ec11-997e-281878b83d8a&ceid=4925535) from Donald Trump. [Brent Waltz](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/44182858/340449721/1822141822?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhZWQ1Y2RlNi1mMGJjLWVjMTEtOTk3ZS0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Jnmu5QrUlFpMiPPQASRyfMxUeq5mU-6QndaLO-UCSVQ=&emci=7234f70d-e8bc-ec11-997e-281878b83d8a&emdi=aed5cde6-f0bc-ec11-997e-281878b83d8a&ceid=4925535) (former State Senator) pled guilty this week to depositing $40,500 in illegal contributions to his failed Congressional campaign. Waltz is faced with jail time and now joined other Republicans like [Mike Braun](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/44182859/340449722/938653524?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhZWQ1Y2RlNi1mMGJjLWVjMTEtOTk3ZS0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Jnmu5QrUlFpMiPPQASRyfMxUeq5mU-6QndaLO-UCSVQ=&emci=7234f70d-e8bc-ec11-997e-281878b83d8a&emdi=aed5cde6-f0bc-ec11-997e-281878b83d8a&ceid=4925535) (U.S. Senator) and [Brian Young](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/44182860/340449723/727164077?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhZWQ1Y2RlNi1mMGJjLWVjMTEtOTk3ZS0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Jnmu5QrUlFpMiPPQASRyfMxUeq5mU-6QndaLO-UCSVQ=&emci=7234f70d-e8bc-ec11-997e-281878b83d8a&emdi=aed5cde6-f0bc-ec11-997e-281878b83d8a&ceid=4925535) (Pulaski County Councilman) who have ignored laws to win elections and fulfill the Indiana GOP’s greater partisan agenda. Young also alleged he informed the previous and current Republican county chairs that he was breaking election law - and they still cleared him to run. In contrast, Indiana Democrats are hard at work delivering the kitchen-table issues most important to Hoosier families. With the [American Rescue Plan](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/44182861/340449724/2075701864?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhZWQ1Y2RlNi1mMGJjLWVjMTEtOTk3ZS0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Jnmu5QrUlFpMiPPQASRyfMxUeq5mU-6QndaLO-UCSVQ=&emci=7234f70d-e8bc-ec11-997e-281878b83d8a&emdi=aed5cde6-f0bc-ec11-997e-281878b83d8a&ceid=4925535) and [The Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act (The Jobs Act)](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/44182862/340449725/2091091387?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhZWQ1Y2RlNi1mMGJjLWVjMTEtOTk3ZS0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Jnmu5QrUlFpMiPPQASRyfMxUeq5mU-6QndaLO-UCSVQ=&emci=7234f70d-e8bc-ec11-997e-281878b83d8a&emdi=aed5cde6-f0bc-ec11-997e-281878b83d8a&ceid=4925535), André Carson, Frank Mrvan, and statehouse Democrats are creating good-paying jobs, supporting Indiana’s law enforcement, funding public education at levels not seen in a decade, expanding broadband access, and revitalizing the state’s infrastructure systems. Democrats are ignoring the Indiana GOP’s partisanship and standing by Hoosiers when it matters most. **\- American Rescue Plan Funds Habitat for Humanity Expansion and Relocation -** Lastly, Democrats celebrated how President Joe Biden’s American Rescue Plan is still delivering for the Hoosier State. This time, an expansion and relocation of [New Albany Clark and Floyd County’s Habitat for Humanity](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/44182863/340449726/-73462203?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhZWQ1Y2RlNi1mMGJjLWVjMTEtOTk3ZS0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Jnmu5QrUlFpMiPPQASRyfMxUeq5mU-6QndaLO-UCSVQ=&emci=7234f70d-e8bc-ec11-997e-281878b83d8a&emdi=aed5cde6-f0bc-ec11-997e-281878b83d8a&ceid=4925535) will happen all thanks to Democrats like U.S. Congressman André Carson and Frank Mrvan. The organization announced it will build a new facility with 7,500 sq. ft. and include warehouse, workshop, classroom and office space. This investment will allow Habitat for Humanity to serve more low to moderate income Hoosiers in Clark and Floyd County. In contrast, the Indiana Republican Party said “NO” to this brighter future. Politicians like U.S. Senator Todd Young voted “NO” on these investments, and in fact, Indiana GOP Chairman Kyle Hupfer called this program by the Indiana Housing Authority “[socialism](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/44182864/340449727/-1180238315?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhZWQ1Y2RlNi1mMGJjLWVjMTEtOTk3ZS0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Jnmu5QrUlFpMiPPQASRyfMxUeq5mU-6QndaLO-UCSVQ=&emci=7234f70d-e8bc-ec11-997e-281878b83d8a&emdi=aed5cde6-f0bc-ec11-997e-281878b83d8a&ceid=4925535)” – claiming this opposition was a “[great campaign to run on](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/44182865/340449728/178131055?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhZWQ1Y2RlNi1mMGJjLWVjMTEtOTk3ZS0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Jnmu5QrUlFpMiPPQASRyfMxUeq5mU-6QndaLO-UCSVQ=&emci=7234f70d-e8bc-ec11-997e-281878b83d8a&emdi=aed5cde6-f0bc-ec11-997e-281878b83d8a&ceid=4925535)”. Democrats will highlight how the Indiana GOP has no plan for Indiana’s future – just a divisive partisan agenda. **\- Indiana Democratic Party is Hiring -** **Regional Organizing Directors** The Indiana Democratic Party is hiring Regional Organizing Directors to to build a stronger party in the field throughout the Hoosier State. The role is ideal for someone looking to be more involved in politics and party building and who wants to dedicate themselves to learning more about the inner workings of campaigns, strategic planning, and voter targeting and contact. These are important roles to building back a fair and equitable two party system of government in Indiana. For full job descriptions and how to apply visit our website at [https://www.indems.org/jobs/](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/44182866/340449729/1076440731?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhZWQ1Y2RlNi1mMGJjLWVjMTEtOTk3ZS0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Jnmu5QrUlFpMiPPQASRyfMxUeq5mU-6QndaLO-UCSVQ=&emci=7234f70d-e8bc-ec11-997e-281878b83d8a&emdi=aed5cde6-f0bc-ec11-997e-281878b83d8a&ceid=4925535) **Spring and Summer Interns** The Indiana Democratic Party is offering a very exciting opportunity for talented and dedicated individuals interested in gaining experience in the fast-paced world of politics. The Party is seeking to fill internship roles at its headquarters in Indianapolis. This is an outstanding opportunity to get hands-on experience working in the office of a state political party and contribute to the success of all of our Democratic candidates.
#### Benefits
* Valuable skills and firsthand insight into the inner workings of the political and legislative process
* Networking with Indiana Democratic Party leaders, activists, staff, candidates, and elected officials
* Enhanced communication and organizational skills
* Work as part of a dedicated team that is interested in your personal development
* This position is a paid internship through a monthly stipend, interns will also be provided with free parking and opportunities for future training, travel, permanent positions, and career advancement in the field.
* We will work with academic institutions if interns are seeking college credit for this internship. Interns must submit the proper paperwork to the Party and their school.

#### _Intern Duties_
Interns will provide administrative support to party staff. Responsibilities may include:
* campaign research
* voter outreach
* data entry
* assistance with local campaign events
* special projects

####
_Qualifications_
Applicants must be:
* passionate about Democratic politics
* trustworthy and responsible
* able to communicate effectively with others
* flexible and willing to help when needed
* able to work on multiple tasks at once

#### _How to Apply_
Interested applicants need to submit their cover letter, resume, and three professional references to Lauren Ganapini at [lauren@indems.org](mailto:lauren@indems.org). ###

The Indiana GOP has no plan for Indiana’s future – just narrow partisanship. \- Indiana GOP Caught Violating Election Laws Three Times in One Year – Despite Partisan Agenda - The State Party also criticized the Indiana Republican Party for blatantly violating election laws three times in just a year. These errors come despite the Indiana GOP during this year’s session and politicians like Todd Rokita from Donald Trump. (former State Senator) pled guilty this week to depositing $40,500 in illegal contributions to his failed Congressional campaign. Waltz is faced with jail time and now joined other Republicans like (U.S. Senator) and (Pulaski County Councilman) who have ignored laws to win elections and fulfill the Indiana GOP’s greater partisan agenda. Young also alleged he informed the previous and current Republican county chairs that he was breaking election law - and they still cleared him to run. In contrast, Indiana Democrats are hard at work delivering the kitchen-table issues most important to Hoosier families. With the and , André Carson, Frank Mrvan, and statehouse Democrats are creating good-paying jobs, supporting Indiana’s law enforcement, funding public education at levels not seen in a decade, expanding broadband access, and revitalizing the state’s infrastructure systems. Democrats are ignoring the Indiana GOP’s partisanship and standing by Hoosiers when it matters most. \- American Rescue Plan Funds Habitat for Humanity Expansion and Relocation - Lastly, Democrats celebrated how President Joe Biden’s American Rescue Plan is still delivering for the Hoosier State. This time, an expansion and relocation of will happen all thanks to Democrats like U.S. Congressman André Carson and Frank Mrvan. The organization announced it will build a new facility with 7,500 sq. ft. and include warehouse, workshop, classroom and office space. This investment will allow Habitat for Humanity to serve more low to moderate income Hoosiers in Clark and Floyd County. In contrast, the Indiana Republican Party said “NO” to this brighter future. Politicians like U.S. Senator Todd Young voted “NO” on these investments, and in fact, Indiana GOP Chairman Kyle Hupfer called this program by the Indiana Housing Authority “” – claiming this opposition was a “”. Democrats will highlight how the Indiana GOP has no plan for Indiana’s future – just a divisive partisan agenda. \- Indiana Democratic Party is Hiring - Regional Organizing Directors The Indiana Democratic Party is hiring Regional Organizing Directors to to build a stronger party in the field throughout the Hoosier State. The role is ideal for someone looking to be more involved in politics and party building and who wants to dedicate themselves to learning more about the inner workings of campaigns, strategic planning, and voter targeting and contact. These are important roles to building back a fair and equitable two party system of government in Indiana. For full job descriptions and how to apply visit our website at Spring and Summer Interns The Indiana Democratic Party is offering a very exciting opportunity for talented and dedicated individuals interested in gaining experience in the fast-paced world of politics. The Party is seeking to fill internship roles at its headquarters in Indianapolis. This is an outstanding opportunity to get hands-on experience working in the office of a state political party and contribute to the success of all of our Democratic candidates. Benefits Valuable skills and firsthand insight into the inner workings of the political and legislative process Networking with Indiana Democratic Party leaders, activists, staff, candidates, and elected officials Enhanced communication and organizational skills Work as part of a dedicated team that is interested in your personal development This position is a paid internship through a monthly stipend, interns will also be provided with free parking and opportunities for future training, travel, permanent positions, and career advancement in the field. We will work with academic institutions if interns are seeking college credit for this internship. Interns must submit the proper paperwork to the Party and their school. Intern Duties Interns will provide administrative support to party staff. Responsibilities may include: campaign research voter outreach data entry assistance with local campaign events special projects Qualifications Applicants must be: passionate about Democratic politics trustworthy and responsible able to communicate effectively with others flexible and willing to help when needed able to work on multiple tasks at once How to Apply Interested applicants need to submit their cover letter, resume, and three professional references to Lauren Ganapini at mailto:lauren@indems.org. ### [content goes here] Indiana Democratic Party 115 W Washington INDLPS, IN 46202 United States Paid for by the Indiana Democratic Party, www.indems.org. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee.

name: Laurie Teper
subject: Poll Workers Needed!!!
body:
Hello Again!
All those people who said they would work the polls, (and EVERYONE who wants to help Courtney win) WE NEED YOU! Stand in front of each of the county polling locations on Election Day and pass out Courtney Geels palm cards. VERY SIMPLE, BUT OH SO IMPORTANT! Preferably 2 people per county per shift. Locations will vary but the shift hours are the same. Below is a sample of the hours we need covered.
Election Day May 17| Tuesday| 6:30am-9:30am
---|---|---
Election Day May 17| Tuesday| 9:30am -12:30pm
Election Day May 17| Tuesday| 12:30pm-4:00pm
Election Day May 17| Tuesday| 4:00pm-7:30pm

**County locations:**
**Durham:**

NEAL MIDDLE SCHOOL - 201 Baptist Rd, Durham
NORTH REGIONAL LIBRARY - 221 Milton Rd, Durham
COLE MILL ROAD CHURCH OF CHRIST - 1617 Cole Mill Rd, Durham

BAHAMA RURITAN CLUB - 8202 Stagville Rd, Bahama

TEMPLE BAPTIST CHURCH - 4504 Sterling Dr, Durham

Granville:
WILTON ELEMENTARY SCHOOL \- 2555 NC HWY 96, Franklinton

Person:
OLD HELENA SCHOOL LUNCHROOM - 295 Helena Moriah Rd, Timberlake

Alamance:
MARVIN B ELEMENTARY SCHOOL - 2235 Delaney Dr, Burlington

ALAMANCE CIVITAN CLUB HOUSE - 3328 Doctor Pickett Rd, Burlington

FELLOWSHIP BAPTIST CHURCH - 2744 Maple Ave. Burlington

MT ZION BAPTIST CHURCH - 3572 West Greensboro-Chapel Hill Rd, Liberty

ST. LUKE'S METHODIST CHURCH - 725 Greenwood Dr, Burlington

Orange:
GRAVELLY MIDDLE SCHOOL - 4801 West Ten Rd, Efland
CALDWELL COMMUNITY BUILDING - 7607 NC HWY 157, Rougemont

We want to fill these up first and THEN focus on the early voting locations from 4/28-5/14. If you live in Orange County but are willing to volunteer in one of the other counties, that is preferable since it is the least conservative county.

Please take this seriously and reply back to me the location and preference of time from the sample listed above and I will plug you in and send you a confirmation.

Thank you from the bottom of my heart and let's do this!

_Fighting for Truth, Justice and Unity Together!_
Laurie Teper
Volunteer Coordinator
Courtney Geels for Congress NC04
Email: [volunteers@courtneygeelsforcongress.com](mailto:volunteers@courtneygeelsforcongress.com)
Cell: (847)212-5651
[courtneygeelsforcongress.com](http://courtneygeelsforcongress.com)

name: Primary Sinema Project
subject: Three reasons we’re sending this email
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY CHANGE FOR ARIZONA 2024 PAC**
body:
| | | [](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953ylv71NjtRp9uAvapwZeG5Fwod7vf2Q19deFkSzINQB7pYO7Ed0TOwyvjuedJ39zpHIgPjZTod5LVCLRGJ5EuVnvDHqljinnhSRx27Y-QsvG6M20ZQaQYFyLcafJBZA0q-Es3gy5ZULTG9cb9DB_ImcIb9KQ2LoRMFo1fz1uq6hj-9z3O7uRjtWlTOKrc0iqYHtQoVdLlAL-cwHz6w-5g-fNQ04CI18C5hOoBaYNdOW0FXfSpATIDpGKVbP48uaGozBs1Qehwo8MVyzmA6Z0O5s2pNhA_Vec1s-g7rwUTM_5QhFJiYYIOfz-C38PYIxv4s/3l8/sKmrC-UhTZONV7Exyly4nw/h0/VCV1zuYHmqFtwwr4xxsCElJW2hM-AQfUath0XCwDY0A)
Peter, We have big plans to primary Kyrsten Sinema. In fact, the money we raise is strategically invested in Arizona grassroots organizations who are laying the groundwork to oust her. **Here are three reasons we’re asking you to[make your first contribution to help us raise $1,000 in grassroots dollars](https://u1584542.ct.sendgrid.net/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953wsEvCNw9RRN8fJ6P2o2MeS71vWbX0MCxV5NfVC52sI901JsIJUNcsc9mZas8P5xUupZNj6zyYs-cKCptXRXmm7ZKCdmGWAJeXZl5YtEvDGJx0_B_gSYbnNxSGi-2t5p2NPSo4ifljlcNfxvugL5L78DGWwWhUJk_6f16ZimnbK1tanY_zkPqzjcTb1csYdetcgw6Bs-PdDlQ8RChfwDqYSx6__7aAngNjvBqmfYJBpYpntxdVpuzwhnC-jdzHwZ0KdIeWqgahvL8Qy8t6L0TirW7lV6ErrWxUJCQswKVuZEYRewn72PRrMbA-HIam93P0/3l8/sKmrC-UhTZONV7Exyly4nw/h1/_a7_NGFBA7wS79z3h-Nm2uxHDoe6BV9Ve2kVPKWuL8c) today, Peter: **
1. **Kyrsten Sinema is now surpassing Joe Manchin as the biggest roadblock to President Biden’s agenda.** Mitch McConnell just told a room of donors that he’s counting on Sinema to stop Democrats from passing major legislation, because it’s Sinema who is obsessed with keeping Trump’s tax scam in place for her corporate donors.
2. **Sinema must be held accountable for dooming Democrats in the midterms.** Less than eight months away from the midterms, Sinema’s obstruction is all but assuring that Democrats will LOSE their Senate majority — and possibly the House.
3. **We can’t wait until a primary challenger jumps in the race.** Beating an incumbent Senator is hard, especially one with millions in the bank, so we need to act NOW to build the infrastructure we’ll need to replace her.

We can’t afford to let Sinema stay in the U.S. Senate, and that’s why we’re so glad you’re with us. clean_body: | | | Peter, We have big plans to primary Kyrsten Sinema. In fact, the money we raise is strategically invested in Arizona grassroots organizations who are laying the groundwork to oust her. Here are three reasons we’re asking you to today, Peter: 1. Kyrsten Sinema is now surpassing Joe Manchin as the biggest roadblock to President Biden’s agenda. Mitch McConnell just told a room of donors that he’s counting on Sinema to stop Democrats from passing major legislation, because it’s Sinema who is obsessed with keeping Trump’s tax scam in place for her corporate donors. 2. Sinema must be held accountable for dooming Democrats in the midterms. Less than eight months away from the midterms, Sinema’s obstruction is all but assuring that Democrats will LOSE their Senate majority — and possibly the House. 3. We can’t wait until a primary challenger jumps in the race. Beating an incumbent Senator is hard, especially one with millions in the bank, so we need to act NOW to build the infrastructure we’ll need to replace her. We can’t afford to let Sinema stay in the U.S. Senate, and that’s why we’re so glad you’re with us. We’re setting a mid-month goal to raise $1,000 in grassroots dollars to keep our work going strong. We’re strategically investing in grassroots organizations on the ground that have already been organizing against Sinema and will be well positioned to defeat her. Beating Kyrsten Sinema starts with raising revenue so we can give those groups a budget. Also, thank you again for your early support — it really does make a huge impact. Keep an eye on your inbox to learn about even more ways you can help us primary Kyrsten Sinema. Thanks, The Primary Sinema team | This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. My field team needs all the help they can get, which is why I must ask if you’ll help us hit our goal. Here’s why: Right now, we’re putting the bulk of our resources towards carrying out an aggressive field operation – we’re trying to get as many votes as possible in these final weeks. That means going far and wide to knock on doors, make phone calls, and meet voters where they’re at. subject: this is not a race between two white guys from Pittsburgh
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Malcolm PAC
body:
“In the Democratic race, participants were split between Lt. Gov. John Fetterman and Malcolm Kenyatta”
#

Folks —
For months now, the press has covered this race as a clash between two white guys from Pittsburgh, completely leaving our huge movement for working families out of the narrative.
But a new focus group of Pennsylvania swing voters from Axios shows that’s dead wrong. Take a look at what they said:
_"In the Democratic race, participants were split between Lt. Gov. John Fetterman and Malcolm Kenyatta. None expressed a preference for Rep. Conor Lamb (D-Pa.)."_
_Axios_ 4.14.22
This focus group confirms what we’ve known all along — that Malcolm has a clear geographic and demographic base that Conor lacks.
But winning this primary and taking our fight for working folks to the United States Senate is not something that Malcolm can do alone. It’s going to take all of us.
We only have 32 days left, and we’re humbly asking for your support to help us make them count. For Malcolm and this team. And for all the working families counting on us to get the job done.
[Please - will you make a $3 contribution to fuel Malcolm’s campaign today? clean_body: | | | Peter, we need your support right now. Here are three reasons why you should chip in today: → Our runoff is in less than 6 weeks. → Henry Cuellar is a corrupt, corporate-backed incumbent who isn’t fighting for South Texas → Every dollar you contribute right now will help us reach more voters and win on May 24th. We can’t afford to slow down on our fundraising. Jessica is not taking a dime from corporate PACs, instead, we’re relying on your support. We don’t want to wake up on May 25th — the morning after the runoff — and wonder if there was anything else we could have done. We have to leave it all on the field. Jessica has a positive vision for South Texas and will actually serve the people she represents. Henry Cuellar is too busy taking campaign checks from corporate PACs and the private prison and fossil fuel industry. Thank you for your support, Team Cisneros | To give by check: PO Box 660 Laredo, TX 78042

name: Sarah Godlewski
subject: Celebrating one year of our campaign (photos)
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Sarah for Wisconsin
body:
One year ago, I officially launched my campaign to defeat Ron Johnson, flip Wisconsin’s Senate seat blue, and expand our Democratic majority. Whether you’ve been with us since the very beginning or just recently joined — I am so grateful for every single person who has gotten us this far. I would like to take a moment to share some of the accomplishments I’m most proud of in our campaign for U.S. Senate. My hope is that they’ll make you proud, too. Ours was the first campaign in the race to receive **grassroots donations from all 72 counties** in Wisconsin — and we just reported **our best fundraising quarter yet!** That’s remarkable, and it means we’re working hard to grow and sustain our momentum in this race. From Superior to Kenosha and everywhere in between, we’ve hosted **roundtable discussions, meet and greets, and other grassroots events all over the state** to speak directly with voters — and it’s clear our message is resonating. (Keep scrolling for a few of my favorite photos!) Over the past few weeks, we’ve rolled out **comprehensive policy plans** focused on **[reinvigorating our rural communities](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/44183104/340450805/511648429?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODI4NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1YzJjNTU2MS1mMmJjLWVjMTEtOTk3ZS0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=qHAxf-Tf_1JvRUkGMJCm9ogbxcGifkUUaUD1f4lSEdE=&emci=25e04e58-f2bc-ec11-997e-281878b83d8a&emdi=5c2c5561-f2bc-ec11-997e-281878b83d8a&ceid=18659593)** , **[reining in Big Pharma](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/44183105/340450806/140418080?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODI4NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1YzJjNTU2MS1mMmJjLWVjMTEtOTk3ZS0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=qHAxf-Tf_1JvRUkGMJCm9ogbxcGifkUUaUD1f4lSEdE=&emci=25e04e58-f2bc-ec11-997e-281878b83d8a&emdi=5c2c5561-f2bc-ec11-997e-281878b83d8a&ceid=18659593)** , and **[cutting costs for working families](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/44183106/340450807/-977189693?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODI4NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1YzJjNTU2MS1mMmJjLWVjMTEtOTk3ZS0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=qHAxf-Tf_1JvRUkGMJCm9ogbxcGifkUUaUD1f4lSEdE=&emci=25e04e58-f2bc-ec11-997e-281878b83d8a&emdi=5c2c5561-f2bc-ec11-997e-281878b83d8a&ceid=18659593)** across the board. We also launched **our first TV ads** of the campaign, and generous contributions from supporters like you have helped us keep them on the air. That’s critical to our success in this race, and I can’t thank our grassroots donors enough for chipping in when they can. It means the world to me. The most recent head-to-head poll shows that I'm the only Democrat who can defeat Ron Johnson. And _The Washington Post_ agrees — reporting that **I’m "the best chance to rid the Senate of Ron Johnson."** Wisconsin will determine Senate control, but beating a two-term incumbent with the full support of Donald Trump, Mitch McConnell, and special interests will not be easy. I'm not taking a dime from corporate PACs. Instead, I'm relying on supporters like you to help us keep going strong. This grassroots team has been by my side for the last year, and I'm hoping I can continue to count on your support during the final months of this campaign. So, I'm personally asking: **[Will you make your first grassroots donation to our U.S. Senate campaign today? I’m in this race to fight for Wisconsin families. Ron Johnson is in it for himself. With your help, we can defeat him, flip this seat, and expand our majority.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/44183107/340450808/-1761982453?refcode=em22_220415_oneyea_fnd_gq_act___sg_ettx&utm_campaign=em22_220415_oneyea_fnd_gq_act___sg_ettx&utm_medium=email&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODI4NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1YzJjNTU2MS1mMmJjLWVjMTEtOTk3ZS0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=qHAxf-Tf_1JvRUkGMJCm9ogbxcGifkUUaUD1f4lSEdE=)** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | [**$5**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/44183108/340450809/-1761982453?refcode=em22_220415_oneyea_fnd_gq_act___sg_ettx&utm_campaign=em22_220415_oneyea_fnd_gq_act___sg_ettx&utm_medium=email&express_lane=true&amount=5&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODI4NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1YzJjNTU2MS1mMmJjLWVjMTEtOTk3ZS0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=qHAxf-Tf_1JvRUkGMJCm9ogbxcGifkUUaUD1f4lSEdE=)

clean_body: One year ago, I officially launched my campaign to defeat Ron Johnson, flip Wisconsin’s Senate seat blue, and expand our Democratic majority. Whether you’ve been with us since the very beginning or just recently joined — I am so grateful for every single person who has gotten us this far. I would like to take a moment to share some of the accomplishments I’m most proud of in our campaign for U.S. Senate. My hope is that they’ll make you proud, too. Ours was the first campaign in the race to receive grassroots donations from all 72 counties in Wisconsin — and we just reported our best fundraising quarter yet! That’s remarkable, and it means we’re working hard to grow and sustain our momentum in this race. From Superior to Kenosha and everywhere in between, we’ve hosted roundtable discussions, meet and greets, and other grassroots events all over the state to speak directly with voters — and it’s clear our message is resonating. (Keep scrolling for a few of my favorite photos!) Over the past few weeks, we’ve rolled out comprehensive policy plans focused on , , and across the board. We also launched our first TV ads of the campaign, and generous contributions from supporters like you have helped us keep them on the air. That’s critical to our success in this race, and I can’t thank our grassroots donors enough for chipping in when they can. It means the world to me. The most recent head-to-head poll shows that I'm the only Democrat who can defeat Ron Johnson. And The Washington Post agrees — reporting that I’m "the best chance to rid the Senate of Ron Johnson." Wisconsin will determine Senate control, but beating a two-term incumbent with the full support of Donald Trump, Mitch McConnell, and special interests will not be easy. I'm not taking a dime from corporate PACs. Instead, I'm relying on supporters like you to help us keep going strong. This grassroots team has been by my side for the last year, and I'm hoping I can continue to count on your support during the final months of this campaign. So, I'm personally asking: = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | = | = | = | = | = | = Thank you so much for the support you've shown me over the last year. Together, we'll be able to defeat Ron Johnson in November. Forward! — Sarah Godlewski P.S. Here are a few of my favorite photos from the trail: = Paid for by Sarah for Wisconsin

name: Team Welch
subject: GOP lawmakers across the country have proposed record anti-LGBTQ+ legislation this year
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY WELCH FOR VERMONT
body:
Electing Peter could be the difference between more discriminatory anti-LGBTQ+ legislation and creating a safe space for our youth to thrive.

# [](https://act.welchforvermont.com/go/144?t=2&akid=246%2E61531%2EmgX50g)
Hello,
Republican lawmakers have launched an all-out, nationwide assault on LGBTQ+ equality this year—with a record number of anti-LGBTQ+ bills, and a particular focus on attacking trans children.
Take a look:
FIRST, in Texas, Governor Gregg Abbott demanded teachers, nurses, doctors, and other licensed professionals to report parents who give their children life-saving gender-affirming care—calling it child abuse. And he threatened criminal legal consequences for those who don’t endanger trans kids by reporting their families to the authorities.
THEN, Iowa Governor Kim Reynolds signed a law that bans trans girls and women in the state from competing on sports teams that match their gender identity.
NOW, Utah lawmakers have also just banned transgender youth athletes from playing on girls teams. The law was too extreme for the Republican governor, who vetoed it and said it was wrong to pass this bill when only four trans kids were playing high school sports in the whole state: “Four kids who are just trying to find some friends and feel like they are a part of something.” But the GOP legislature overrode his veto, making Utah the 12th state with a ban like this on the books.
Criminalizing gender-affirming care is clearly discriminatory, and it damages the mental health of trans children who are already vulnerable. Not only do actions like this jeopardize safe spaces, they also enlist the public as vigilantes to do the bidding of conservative lawmakers.
**Peter will never stop fighting for the rights of all Americans, including the LGBTQ+ community.** He’s building a Vermont for all of us. He helped pass the Equality Act in the House as a co-sponsor, and he’ll fight to get it over the finish line as senator. But if Republicans flip this targeted seat and take back the Senate, Mitch McConnell will make sure there is never a vote to protect LGBTQ+ rights.
It’s never been more important to elect a progressive like Peter to the Senate. This race could be the difference between more discriminatory anti-LGBTQ+ legislation and creating a safe space for our youth to thrive.
[**If you’re all in to fight back against discriminatory legislation, add your name right away.**](https://act.welchforvermont.com/signup/em-fight-discriminatory-lgbtq-laws/?source=em_20220415_f_t_ftn&t=3&akid=246%2E61531%2EmgX50g)
Thank you,
Team Welch

---
| [DONATE ](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pwfv-em-22apr?refcode=em_20220415_f_t_ftn&refcode2=246_61531_mgX50g&akid=246%2E61531%2EmgX50g)
---

---

# Join Ed for Cinco de Mayo
_**Hola!**_ Celebrate Cinco de Mayo with Ed Thelander for Congress at the Patio at 1578. Enjoy complimentary Mexican hors d’oeuvres and margaritas at the cash bar. Cinco de Mayo commemorates Mexico’s victory over the French Empire at the Battle of Puebla in 1862. This November 2022 will commemorate Ed’s victory over his incumbent opponent. Don’t miss this opportunity to meet Ed and hear his campaign strategy. We look forward to seeing you there!
**May 5th** 5:00pm -7:00pm**The Patio at 1578** 1578 State Route 129South Bristol, ME 04568[1578: Maine Events, Catering & Experiences (experience1578.com)](https://thelanderforcongress.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=035bf0620f01206429344ca39&id=6c7f19a336&e=f8a0a76a2b)

subject: I have an urgent personal request
disclaimer_text: Paid for and approved by Charlie Crist, Democrat for Governor
body:
I know you have a busy schedule so I’ll get right to the point. |
---

| | Friend,

I know you have a busy schedule, so I’ll get right to the point. There are just a few hours left until our critical April mid-month deadline, and we need just a bit more help to reach our $15,000 goal. **_[Can you make a donation to help us get there?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/44189106/340478355/1278717326?&amount=5&refcode=20220415_f_3&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTYxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0OWQ0MTAwZS0xNmJkLWVjMTEtOTk3ZS0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Xqa3a537aMw36ytng4LPWqgl4cIkRMBBsFWcTZflIoE=)_**

Charlie

name: MarkKelly.com [via unitedemocrats.org]
subject: question for Peter
disclaimer_text: Paid for by UNITEDemocrats PAC
body:
There’s only one way we overcome the enormous amount of outside spending targeting Mark ... and that’s together.

| [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/44189108/340478359/-1872862983?amount=3&refcode=UD_20220415_EMSPL_E2_T0_AC&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNS8xLzgxNTI4IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjRhZDQxMDBlLTE2YmQtZWMxMS05OTdlLTI4MTg3OGI4M2Q4YSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=RDLSMuRY-R11jFWXwtbsuhnuZ8WL59z8QSlvDVhlSck=) Peter,

Quick, but serious question:

If you knew contributing just $3 would mean Mark Kelly would get re-elected in November, wouldn’t you donate it for sure?

## Humbly Asking
Friend— First and foremost, thank you. I know we’ve sent a number of fundraising emails this week, and I appreciate you opening this one. What most people don’t know about our campaign is that most of the donations we receive come from people like you, chipping in what you can when you can. And that most of those donations come in response to emails like this one. **_[So, I am humbly asking for you to make an investment in me today](https://AudreyHenson.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=f5e3b0f2cd656565b5a883b28&id=0d3fc930f6&e=980b3e1cf3) to help us reach our mid-month goal._**

Audrey Henson

name: Team Beshear
subject: Why Andy vetoed giving himself a raise:
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ANDY BESHEAR FOR GOVERNOR
body:
Republicans legislators just gave themselves a raise while voting against raises for teachers and raising the minimum wage. A lot has happened as the legislative session comes to a close in Frankfort, so we wanted to make sure you didn't miss this, Peter.

**Republicans passed a legislative budget last month that gave themselves pay raises – while giving teachers nothing.** This is unacceptable and just plain wrong.

Look, legislators already earn more than double what the average Kentuckian makes despite only working part-time. They shouldn't be able to give themselves raises while our educators and other hardworking Kentuckians are struggling to get by.

That's why Andy took action and vetoed these raises. He not only vetoed raises for legislators, he vetoed a raise for himself because he believes we should instead be investing in public education by funding universal pre-K and teacher pay raises. It's simply the right thing to do. **But Republicans once again put partisan politics ahead of what's best for Kentuckians and overrode Andy's veto.**

Andy is working every day to put our educators, children, and public schools first. But he needs your help in the fight to ensure our schools have the funding they need and teachers receive the pay they deserve.

**[Will you chip in $5 or more before our mid-month fundraising deadline to help Andy make progress for Kentucky educators, students, and public schools?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/44183948/340456054/-1274636763?refcode=em22_220415_mm1_fnd_gq_act___tb_ettx&utm_campaign=em22_220415_mm1_fnd_gq_act___tb_ettx&utm_medium=email&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85MzM0NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlMDdhYmNhNC1mNmJjLWVjMTEtOTk3ZS0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=CU8WnySXlyMtQ3Tjqf29viCJ1UePf4U7S5hHzQUehyw=)**

Thanks,

Team Beshear | | **Andy is fighting to make the Bluegrass State the best place it can be for generations to come, and he needs you on board. Will you chip in today to Andy's re-election campaign?** [DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/44183949/340456055/-1274636763?refcode=em22_220415_mm1_fnd_gq_act___tb_ettx&utm_campaign=em22_220415_mm1_fnd_gq_act___tb_ettx&utm_medium=email&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85MzM0NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlMDdhYmNhNC1mNmJjLWVjMTEtOTk3ZS0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=CU8WnySXlyMtQ3Tjqf29viCJ1UePf4U7S5hHzQUehyw=)

name: Doug White
subject: Strength in Diversity - Fuerza en la diversidad.
body:
Una casa dividida no aguanta y esto lo vemos cada día más claro. Pasamos de una nación de espíritu y prop&oacute
| | **Can't see this message?_[View in a browser](https://www.dougwhite4congress.us/so/bbO0kIu87/c?w=7i9fABaLPkh6fl2GbDbO8eTzMECIb7NTymJt6gdAb54.eyJ1IjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuZG91Z3doaXRlNGNvbmdyZXNzLnVzL3NvL2JiTzBrSXU4Nz9sYW5ndWFnZVRhZz1lbiIsIm0iOiJtYWlsIiwiYyI6IjA0OTZmZmUwLWMwYmEtNDYwNi05OTg2LWMyODAzN2U4ZDE1NSJ9)_**
---
| | |
---
| | Strength in Diversity - Fuerza en la diversidad.
---
| |
---
| |
---
| | Una casa dividida no aguanta y esto lo vemos cada día más claro. Pasamos de una nación de espíritu y propósito unidos a una en la que la política se usa para garantizar que no podamos encontrar puntos en común. Para que podamos avanzar como distrito y nación, debemos trabajar juntos. La discusión y el desacuerdo son una parte normal del proceso para lograr grandes cosas. Es cómo se prueban las ideas, se gana apoyo y se logran grandes hazañas. Como nación, nunca debemos estar en oposición, sino a menudo en desacuerdo. Me preocupa mucho que los partidos políticos estén destruyendo nuestro sentido de comunidad y nuestra forma de trabajar juntos. Parece que las partes están trabajando para distanciarnos aún más y ahora, en lugar de estar en desacuerdo sobre un aspecto de un problema, ahora descartamos los aportes sumariamente en función de la lealtad del partido. Agrupamos todos los prejuicios en cada uno de sus pensamientos y declaraciones, por lo que cancelamos escuchar cualquier aporte que pueda ser valioso para "nuestro" éxito. En lugar de gastar energía para reconstruir nuestras comunidades y trabajar juntos por el bien común, veo duplicar la división y eliminar oportunidades para unirnos. El último ciclo electoral, los miembros del Partido Republicano en Yakima se basaron en el debate tradicional de la Liga de Mujeres Votantes. En cambio, optaron por mantener su propio debate consigo mismos. Una persona no puede tener un debate consigo mismo y con otros que piensan exactamente lo mismo; esta es una cámara de eco con el resultado de alejarse más de un objetivo común a una posición más extrema y opuesta. Ahora vemos que la RNC rechaza por unanimidad el proceso de debate presidencial. El único momento en que el pueblo estadounidense puede ver a ambos candidatos en el mismo escenario para que puedan tomar decisiones imparciales libres de promoción falsa o retórica partidista. Haré todo lo que pueda para asegurarme de que volvamos a encaminarnos como una comunidad unida a un futuro que beneficie a todos. A house divided can not stand and we see this clearer every passing day. We went from a nation of joined spirit and purpose to one in which politics is used to ensure we can not find common ground. For us to move forward as a district and a nation we must work together. Discussion, disagreement and even arguments are a normal part of the process to accomplish great things. It is how ideas are tested, support is gained and great deeds are accomplished. As a nation we should never be in opposition, but often in disagreement. I am gravely troubled that political parties are destroying our sense of community and way of working together. It seems the parties are working to drive us further apart and now rather than disagree on an aspect of an issue we now dismiss input summarily based on party loyalty. We lump all the prejudice into their every thought and statement thereby canceling hearing any input that may be valuable to “our” success. Rather than energy being spent to rebuild our communities and working together for the greater good, I see doubling down on divisiveness and removing opportunities to come together. The last election cycle, members of the Republican party in Yakima drew from the long standing League of Women Voters debate. They instead chose to hold their own debate with themselves. You can’t have a debate with yourself and others that think exactly the same; this is an echo chamber with the result being moving further away from a common goal to a more extreme and opposite position. We now see the RNC unanimously rejecting the Presidential debate process. The one time when the American people can see both candidates on the same stage so that they can make unbiased decisions free from false promotion or party rhetoric. I will do everything I can to make sure that we get back on track as a community that is united to a future that benefits all.
---

---
| |
---
|

| | El Museo REACH en Richland fue sede de nuestro Ayuntamiento. Este hermoso espacio atrajo a una buena multitud. El tema candente fue la inmigración y cómo lograr que los procesos de visa sean más accesibles y equitativos para agricultores y trabajadores.
---
| The REACH Museum in Richland was host to our Town Hall. This beautiful space brought a good crowd. The hot topic was immigration and how to get visa processes to be more accessible equitable for farmers and workers.

|

| | Door knocking in Richland. We visited 416 homes and will reach 50,000+ before the primary. Come join us any Saturday; we always have cookies. More information CampaignManager@DougWhite4Congress.us

| Llamando a la puerta en Richland. Visitamos 416 hogares y llegaremos a más de 50,000 antes de las primarias. Ven y únete a nosotros cualquier sábado; siempre tenemos galletas. Más información CampaignManager@DougWhite4Congress.us

|

| | Mark Figueroa, nuestro Coordinador de Diversidad, ha estado quemando el cuero del zapato. Se está reuniendo con líderes empresariales y personas de las comunidades desde Okanogan, reuniéndose con personas de la reserva de Colville, la comunidad latina en Tonasket hasta White Salmon en el condado de Klickitat. Tenemos grupos de escucha, toca puertas, fiestas comunitarias y más planificadas casi todos los días de cada semana. Consulte nuestros próximos eventos en nuestro [sitio web](https://www.dougwhite4congress.us/so/bbO0kIu87/c?w=LA1N70w-c9h6LPMV_qAgX5hcJGTb9080vagcN57jTFM.eyJ1IjoiaHR0cDovL2RvdWd3aGl0ZTRjb25ncmVzcy51cyIsInIiOiJiZjQ1MDkxYS0yMDliLTQxYjYtODU2OS1mODNhNjBjMzczMGQiLCJtIjoibWFpbCIsImMiOiIwNDk2ZmZlMC1jMGJhLTQ2MDYtOTk4Ni1jMjgwMzdlOGQxNTUifQ).

He is meeting with business leaders and people of the communities from Okanogan meeting people of the Colville reservation, latino community in Tonasket all the way down to White Salmon in Klickitat county. We have listening groups, door knocking, community parties and more planned almost every day of every week. Check out our upcoming events on our [website](https://www.dougwhite4congress.us/so/bbO0kIu87/c?w=49nzo3_RjkxnsEJTS3__UYq-zQx0VPiboiRCs7AJBkk.eyJ1IjoiaHR0cDovL2RvdWd3aGl0ZTRjb25ncmVzcy51cyIsInIiOiIyNWVjODk2Yy02NmZlLTQ4MDktZjA1Ni02YjNiNjAzNjI3MjEiLCJtIjoibWFpbCIsImMiOiIwNDk2ZmZlMC1jMGJhLTQ2MDYtOTk4Ni1jMjgwMzdlOGQxNTUifQ).

Una persona no puede tener un debate consigo mismo y con otros que piensan exactamente lo mismo; esta es una cámara de eco con el resultado de alejarse más de un objetivo común a una posición más extrema y opuesta. Ahora vemos que la RNC rechaza por unanimidad el proceso de debate presidencial. El único momento en que el pueblo estadounidense puede ver a ambos candidatos en el mismo escenario para que puedan tomar decisiones imparciales libres de promoción falsa o retórica partidista. Haré todo lo que pueda para asegurarme de que volvamos a encaminarnos como una comunidad unida a un futuro que beneficie a todos. A house divided can not stand and we see this clearer every passing day. We went from a nation of joined spirit and purpose to one in which politics is used to ensure we can not find common ground. For us to move forward as a district and a nation we must work together. Discussion, disagreement and even arguments are a normal part of the process to accomplish great things. It is how ideas are tested, support is gained and great deeds are accomplished. As a nation we should never be in opposition, but often in disagreement. I am gravely troubled that political parties are destroying our sense of community and way of working together. It seems the parties are working to drive us further apart and now rather than disagree on an aspect of an issue we now dismiss input summarily based on party loyalty. We lump all the prejudice into their every thought and statement thereby canceling hearing any input that may be valuable to “our” success. Rather than energy being spent to rebuild our communities and working together for the greater good, I see doubling down on divisiveness and removing opportunities to come together. The last election cycle, members of the Republican party in Yakima drew from the long standing League of Women Voters debate. They instead chose to hold their own debate with themselves. You can’t have a debate with yourself and others that think exactly the same; this is an echo chamber with the result being moving further away from a common goal to a more extreme and opposite position. We now see the RNC unanimously rejecting the Presidential debate process. The one time when the American people can see both candidates on the same stage so that they can make unbiased decisions free from false promotion or party rhetoric. I will do everything I can to make sure that we get back on track as a community that is united to a future that benefits all. | | | | | | | El Museo REACH en Richland fue sede de nuestro Ayuntamiento. Este hermoso espacio atrajo a una buena multitud. El tema candente fue la inmigración y cómo lograr que los procesos de visa sean más accesibles y equitativos para agricultores y trabajadores. | The REACH Museum in Richland was host to our Town Hall. This beautiful space brought a good crowd. The hot topic was immigration and how to get visa processes to be more accessible equitable for farmers and workers. | | | | | Door knocking in Richland. We visited 416 homes and will reach 50,000+ before the primary. Come join us any Saturday; we always have cookies. More information CampaignManager@DougWhite4Congress.us | Llamando a la puerta en Richland. Visitamos 416 hogares y llegaremos a más de 50,000 antes de las primarias. Ven y únete a nosotros cualquier sábado; siempre tenemos galletas. Más información CampaignManager@DougWhite4Congress.us | | | | | Mark Figueroa, nuestro Coordinador de Diversidad, ha estado quemando el cuero del zapato. Se está reuniendo con líderes empresariales y personas de las comunidades desde Okanogan, reuniéndose con personas de la reserva de Colville, la comunidad latina en Tonasket hasta White Salmon en el condado de Klickitat. Tenemos grupos de escucha, toca puertas, fiestas comunitarias y más planificadas casi todos los días de cada semana. Consulte nuestros próximos eventos en nuestro . | Mark Figueroa our Diversity Advocate has been burning the shoe leather. He is meeting with business leaders and people of the communities from Okanogan meeting people of the Colville reservation, latino community in Tonasket all the way down to White Salmon in Klickitat county. We have listening groups, door knocking, community parties and more planned almost every day of every week. Check out our upcoming events on our . | | | | It will take all of us to win! Please volunteer | | | | | | | | Share on social | | | | You've received this email because you are a subscriber of . If you feel you received it by mistake or wish to unsubscribe, please _.

name: Demings HQ
subject: just a couple more donations
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Val Demings for U
body:
Val Demings is gaining ground on Marco Rubio
| | |
---
**Friend, time is running out to help Val Demings hit her Mid Month goal – and she needs just 234 more donations to close the gap. In a race that will determine Senate control, missing this goal is not an option. **
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/44189494/340483020/-892300846?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&refcode=jtk2797-fr-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84Mzc4NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4YmU1ZDRkOC0xOGJkLWVjMTEtOTk3ZS0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=2lwuNAiZdUEtuX4ANUw0rQoNKIFJQIlOcoT6nE_K65c=)
Grassroots support has made our race competitive. It's why The Miami Times says she's closing in. So we're not exaggerating when we say the consequences of missing this goal are serious. She's gaining ground on Marco Rubio, and it's because of people like you helping her meet her goals, friend.
**But with Election Day only months away, GOP megadonors are taking notice of her momentum and pouring MILLIONS into Florida to prop up Rubio. If she misses tonight's goal, we risk increasing their chances of flipping the Senate. We _ cannot let that happen._**
With a grassroots team this strong, we know that if we dig deep, we _will_ flip Florida blue. But that means hitting every last fundraising goal between now and Election Day – including tomorrow's essential deadline. **T****he only way Val Demings can win against the GOP dark-money machine is with your support.**
**[_Val Demings is so close to reaching her Mid Month fundraising goal but she still needs 234 more donations to help close the gap. In a race that will determine Senate control, missing this goal is not an option. = Grassroots support has made our race competitive. It's why The Miami Times says she's closing in. So we're not exaggerating when we say the consequences of missing this goal are serious. She's gaining ground on Marco Rubio, and it's because of people like you helping her meet her goals, friend. But with Election Day only months away, GOP megadonors are taking notice of her momentum and pouring MILLIONS into Florida to prop up Rubio. If she misses tonight's goal, we risk increasing their chances of flipping the Senate. We cannot let that happen. With a grassroots team this strong, we know that if we dig deep, we will flip Florida blue. name: Lorraine
subject: Re: Poll Workers Needed!!!
body:
I live 3 miles from Alamance Civitan Club (my voting precinct - very very small location...so you may only need 1 person per shift here), 8 minutes from Fellowship Baptist Church and under 10 minutes from Melvin Smith Elementary School. I can work the 4-7:30 shift at any location. Let me know where you need me!

Lorraine

> On Apr 15, 2022, at 7:21 PM, Laurie Teper <volunteers@courtneygeelsforcongress.com> wrote:
>
>
>
> Hello Again!
> All those people who said they would work the polls, (and EVERYONE who wants to help Courtney win) WE NEED YOU! Stand in front of each of the county polling locations on Election Day and pass out Courtney Geels palm cards. VERY SIMPLE, BUT OH SO IMPORTANT! Preferably 2 people per county per shift. Locations will vary but the shift hours are the same. Below is a sample of the hours we need covered.
> Election Day May 17| Tuesday| 6:30am-9:30am
> ---|---|---
> Election Day May 17| Tuesday| 9:30am -12:30pm
> Election Day May 17| Tuesday| 12:30pm-4:00pm
> Election Day May 17| Tuesday| 4:00pm-7:30pm
>
> **County locations:**
> **Durham:**
>
> NEAL MIDDLE SCHOOL - 201 Baptist Rd, Durham
> NORTH REGIONAL LIBRARY - 221 Milton Rd, Durham
> COLE MILL ROAD CHURCH OF CHRIST - 1617 Cole Mill Rd, Durham
>
> BAHAMA RURITAN CLUB - 8202 Stagville Rd, Bahama
>
> TEMPLE BAPTIST CHURCH - 4504 Sterling Dr, Durham
>
>
>
> Granville:
> WILTON ELEMENTARY SCHOOL - 2555 NC HWY 96, Franklinton
>
>
>
> Person:
> OLD HELENA SCHOOL LUNCHROOM - 295 Helena Moriah Rd, Timberlake
>
>
> Alamance:
> MARVIN B ELEMENTARY SCHOOL - 2235 Delaney Dr, Burlington
>
> ALAMANCE CIVITAN CLUB HOUSE - 3328 Doctor Pickett Rd, Burlington
>
> FELLOWSHIP BAPTIST CHURCH - 2744 Maple Ave. Burlington
>
> MT ZION BAPTIST CHURCH - 3572 West Greensboro-Chapel Hill Rd, Liberty
>
> ST. LUKE'S METHODIST CHURCH - 725 Greenwood Dr, Burlington
>
>
>
> Orange:
> GRAVELLY MIDDLE SCHOOL - 4801 West Ten Rd, Efland
> CALDWELL COMMUNITY BUILDING - 7607 NC HWY 157, Rougemont
>
>
>
> We want to fill these up first and THEN focus on the early voting locations from 4/28-5/14. If you live in Orange County but are willing to volunteer in one of the other counties, that is preferable since it is the least conservative county.
>
>
> Please take this seriously and reply back to me the location and preference of time from the sample listed above and I will plug you in and send you a confirmation.
>
>
>
> Thank you from the bottom of my heart and let's do this!
>
>
> _Fighting for Truth, Justice and Unity Together!_
> Laurie Teper
> Volunteer Coordinator
> Courtney Geels for Congress NC04
> Email: [volunteers@courtneygeelsforcongress.com](mailto:volunteers@courtneygeelsforcongress.com)

>

name: Mark Walker
subject: Blessings on this Good Friday
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Walker4NC
body:
A weekend of celebration and reflection
| | | [](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/5dffe9cf8697fc0a3a89ac0c84d83476/35903/e00ffe5dc41fbd70/fe52f9f187dfda9bc3d6b838967d376c/35903)
---
Peter, Kelly and I wanted to send a brief message wishing you a blessed Good Friday and a joy-filled Easter weekend. This season is about more than egg hunts and bunnies. It is a time to reflect on the sacrificial love of Jesus Christ and prepare for Easter Sunday when we will celebrate his resurrection. With grateful hearts we are reminded that we have hope - not in this broken world, but in our Savior, Jesus Christ.
---
---
Jesus said, “I am the resurrection and the life. He who believes in me, though he may die, he shall live.” – John 11:25 We hope you have a blessed weekend celebrating Jesus’ love with family! Mark and Kelly Walker
---

name: Durbin HQ
subject: Here’s how we continue making history, Peter
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Dick Durbin
body:
We can’t do this alone
#
Peter,
Already this year, Sen. Durbin and Senate Democrats have secured hard-fought victories, like the historic confirmation of Justice Ketanji Brown Jackson.
This was made possible by grassroots supporters like you, Peter, and the efforts of so many who helped us flip the Senate blue.
But now, Mitch McConnell and the GOP won’t commit to holding any more Supreme Court hearings if they retake the Senate–a signal that they’re positioning themselves to _again_ obstruct the Supreme Court confirmation process and make historic victories like the one we just secured virtually impossible.
**[We can’t let them. Will you rush in a $5contribution now to help us meet our mid-month goal and stay on track to defend and expand our Senate majority this November?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/44184381/340459180/-1716504305?refcode=dd_em_220415_1&amount=5&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&addr1=&city=Englewood&state=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODQ4OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2MDkzNTBkZC1mOWJjLWVjMTEtOTk3ZS0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=hjBR2FUUCKbI4aKRyAMeBQYSSlAXd1qLUf9TuHcKztc=)**
It’s just the latest sign of what’s to come if Republicans retake the Senate: more obstruction and partisan games that pose a serious risk to our pandemic recovery and working families everywhere.
There’s still so much left for Senate Democrats to accomplish, like bringing down prescription drug costs, fixing our broken immigration system, and more.
**[We can’t and won’t stop here, but we need your help to stay on track and win in November. clean_body: We can’t do this alone Peter, Already this year, Sen. Durbin and Senate Democrats have secured hard-fought victories, like the historic confirmation of Justice Ketanji Brown Jackson. This was made possible by grassroots supporters like you, Peter, and the efforts of so many who helped us flip the Senate blue. But now, Mitch McConnell and the GOP won’t commit to holding any more Supreme Court hearings if they retake the Senate–a signal that they’re positioning themselves to again obstruct the Supreme Court confirmation process and make historic victories like the one we just secured virtually impossible. = It’s just the latest sign of what’s to come if Republicans retake the Senate: more obstruction and partisan games that pose a serious risk to our pandemic recovery and working families everywhere. There’s still so much left for Senate Democrats to accomplish, like bringing down prescription drug costs, fixing our broken immigration system, and more. = Thank you, Durbin HQ **= Paid for by Friends of Dick Durbin

**We need to show our Trump-backed opponent just how strong this movement is, but falling short of goals like this will put our entire campaign in serious danger. The stakes are getting higher every day. This race is already ranked as one of the most competitive open-seat contests of 2022, and the worst of the GOP attacks are still yet to come. Republicans are desperate to keep Arizona red, and they'll spend _millions_ to hold us back. Defeating them has to start NOW. We can't wait until we are closer to the election.
**[So please, donate $5 or whatever you can spare before this crucial mid-month deadline to help close our fundraising gap, defeat GOP extremism and flip Arizona blue.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/44189639/340491173/1742512187?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&refcode=jtk611-fr-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS84OTQ2MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2ZGY0ZDcwYi0xZGJkLWVjMTEtOTk3ZS0yODE4NzhiODNkOGEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=1FvyEp7U7vLRrgiLN3EoFGBI4t9a3CyVHc4pZYlt8lc=)**

Marco Lopez
